name: Rajib Mondal
bio: Rajib is an electrical engineer and medical student passionate about conducting research in neurodegenerative diseases, gut health, and neural interfaces to improve patient care. He joined the Harvard-MIT Health-Sciences and Technology department for the Medical Engineering and Medical Physics program in 2020 after completing his M.Sc. and B.Sc. in Electrical Engineering and Information Technology focusing on biomedical applications at RWTH Aachen University, Germany. In 2020 he finished the second step of the German federal medical examination at Heinrich-Heine University Duesseldorf. Rajib’s research focuses on gut-brain communication in the context of Parkinson’s disease as well as neuroimmunity in the context of inflammatory bowel disease. Besides doing research, Rajib enjoys the four Cs: concerts, cooking, cocktails, & community.
